title: Upheaval at the Ethereum Foundation Has Some of Crypto’s Biggest Names Feeling Bullish
summary: The Ethereum Foundation laid off 20% of its staff while a new spinoff group, EthLabs, launched this week. Many crypto leaders see these changes as a sign of Ethereum's growing maturity and decentralization.
body:
The Ethereum Foundation, the key nonprofit supporting the Ethereum blockchain, laid off 20% of its staff this week. The move is part of an effort to streamline operations and reduce reliance on centralized control. At the same time, a new spinoff organization called EthLabs has launched, backed by several prominent crypto figures. EthLabs is intended to take over some of the Foundation’s development responsibilities, operating independently and focusing on community-driven innovation.

While the layoffs are significant, they have not dampened the spirits of several high-profile crypto leaders. Some view the restructuring as a positive step toward greater decentralization, suggesting that Ethereum is maturing and becoming less dependent on a single entity. EthLabs, with its independent governance and fresh mandate, is seen as a vehicle to accelerate development and attract new talent.

For everyday users, these changes could lead to faster innovation and more decentralized projects on the Ethereum network. The job cuts, though painful for those affected, are being interpreted by some analysts as a strategic adjustment for long-term health. The launch of EthLabs may result in new tools, upgrades, and applications that directly benefit users.

Looking ahead, the crypto community will be closely watching how these changes affect Ethereum's development pace and overall decentralization. Users should monitor EthLabs' initial projects and any announcements from the Ethereum Foundation about future restructuring. The next few months will be critical in determining the lasting impact of this upheaval.
twitterThread: Big shake-up at the Ethereum Foundation: 20% layoffs, but some of crypto’s biggest names are feeling bullish. Why?

The Ethereum Foundation is cutting staff to push toward decentralization. Meanwhile, a new spinoff group called EthLabs launched this week, backed by top crypto figures, to take over development and drive community-driven innovation.

What does this mean for you? Expect more decentralized projects, faster upgrades, and potential new tools. Keep an eye on EthLabs' progress. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-24-ethereum-foundation-layoffs-spark-bullish-sentiment-among-crypto-leaders
